channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mumbai Family Dies Due to Zinc Phosphide Poisoning, Investigation Underway
item.description: In Mumbai, four members of the Dokadia family tragically died within hours of each other after consuming watermelon at their home in Pydhonie. Initially suspected to be food poisoning, the case took a...

item.title: Pakistan Govt clarifies UAE deportation reports as routine and unfounded
item.description: The Government of Pakistan on Friday officially rejected recent social media reports claiming targeted deportations of Pakistani nationals from the United Arab Emirates. The Interior Ministry clarifie...

item.title: Ben Bishop Discusses Improving Independent Wrestling with Basic Amenities and Time Management
item.description: Independent wrestling star Ben Bishop, known for his towering 7-foot presence, recently shared insights on ways to improve the indie wrestling scene. Speaking to Fox News Digital, Bishop emphasized th...

item.title: 54 Police Trainees Dismissed from Kiganjo Training School Over Pregnancy, Forged Documents and Other Issues
item.description: In Nyeri, the National Police Service (NPS) has dismissed a total of 54 police trainees from the Kiganjo Training School following various integrity breaches. Among the dismissed recruits, 18 were pre...

item.title: Saskatchewan rural municipalities struggle with flood repair costs as waters remain high
item.description: Severe flooding across Saskatchewan has caused significant damage to roads and bridges in many rural municipalities (RMs), prompting concerns about the financial and logistical challenges of repairs. ...

item.title: Russia scales back Victory Day parade as the war in Ukraine drags on
item.description: Russia’s 2026 Victory Day celebration is expected to be notably smaller than usual, with no display of tanks or missiles in Moscow for the first time in nearly two decades. The pared-down parade refle...

item.title: Calgary Community Organizes Fundraiser for Teen Football Player Battling Cancer
item.description: Kane Hart, a 17-year-old Calgary high school football player, has been sidelined from the sport he loves after being diagnosed with stage 4 lymphoma in February. Football has been a major part of Kane...

item.title: Five dead, 28 injured in Iringa bus and lorry collision
item.description: Early morning on Friday, May 8, 2026, a serious road accident happened at Tanangozi area in Mseke Ward, Iringa Region. A Yutong passenger bus going from Dodoma to Mbeya collided head-on with a FAW lor...

item.title: Airtel Africa sees strong profit growth driven by data and mobile money
item.description: Airtel Africa don announce say their net profit don rise sharply by 147.4% for last year compared to 2024. Dis big increase na because their total revenue grow by 29.5%, reaching $6.4 billion from $5 ...

item.title: Conservative Leader Poilievre Faces Challenges at Canada Strong and Free Network Conference
item.description: At the 2026 Canada Strong and Free Network (CFSN) conference in Ottawa, Conservative leader Pierre Poilievre had a critical opportunity to reinvigorate his party amid a challenging political landscape...

item.title: Tanzania’s Nicolene Viljoen qualifies for 200m butterfly final in Africa Championships
item.description: Tanzania swimmer Nicolene Viljoen continues to shine at the Africa Aquatics Championships in Algeria after qualifying for the final of the women’s 200m butterfly. In the heats, she swam with calm and ...

item.title: Akhilesh Yadav expresses support for TMC and DMK, critiques Congress in post-election context
item.description: On 8th May 2026, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav publicly expressed support for Trinamool Congress (TMC) and DMK following their recent defeats in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu assembly elections. S...

item.title: Zanzibar Government Pushes Faster and Coordinated Digital Systems to Improve Services and Revenue
item.description: President Hussein Mwinyi of Zanzibar has directed the government to speed up digital transformation as part of strengthening the economy and improving public service delivery. Speaking through the Min...

item.title: Richard Lewer Wins Archibald Prize for Portrait of Pitjantjatjara Elder
item.description: Melbourne artist Richard Lewer has won the prestigious $100,000 Archibald Prize for portraiture with his synthetic polymer painting of Pitjantjatjara elder and senior artist Iluwanti Ken. The Art Gall...

item.title: Study shows traditional financial advisors remain trusted as finfluencers supplement rather than replace advice
item.description: A recent report by the Securities and Investment Management Association (SIMA) indicates that while more Canadians, especially younger, educated, higher-income, and self-directed investors, are turnin...

item.title: Senate rescinds amendments on leadership eligibility for presiding officers
item.description: On Thursday, di Nigerian Senate rescinded some changes wey dem make to dia Standing Orders after plenty lawmakers shout say e dey conflict with 1999 Constitution as e don be. Earlier for di week, Sena...

item.title: Middlesbrough lodge EFL complaint over alleged spying by Southampton ahead of play-off semi-final
item.description: Middlesbrough FC have lodged a formal complaint with the English Football League (EFL) after allegations emerged that a Southampton staff member engaged in unauthorised filming of their training sessi...

item.title: AI Solutions Aim to Reduce Administrative Delays in Specialist Referrals
item.description: The article explores a persistent problem in healthcare: patients often face long delays or no response at all when trying to schedule appointments with specialists. The root cause is not a shortage o...

item.title: Review: 'Daadi Ki Shaadi' explores family dynamics more than humour
item.description: Ashish R Mohan’s film 'Daadi Ki Shaadi' stars Neetu Kapoor, Kapil Sharma, Sadia Khateeb and R Sarathkumar, presenting a story about a grandmother, Vimla, who decides to remarry, causing a stir in her ...

item.title: Interior Ministry Clarifies There Are No Targeted Deportations of Pakistanis from UAE
item.description: On Friday, the Interior Ministry of Pakistan officially denied social media and media reports claiming targeted deportations of Pakistani nationals from the United Arab Emirates. The ministry emphasis...
